source.method: Playwright browser, fetched JSON via internal /api/v2/dishes/{id}/restaurants endpoint after extracting dish ID 548 from /api/search/autocomplete

notes: TasteAtlas /new-york-style-pizza/wheretoeat is JS-rendered (Angular SPA). The raw HTML is an empty shell; restaurant data is fetched client-side from /api/v2/dishes/548/restaurants. Additionally, the wheretoeat page exhibits aggressive auto-redirects to other pizza ranking pages (pizza-napoletana, chicago-pizza, pizza-al-taglio, traditional-italian-pizza, apizza) within ~1-3 seconds of load, making direct DOM scraping unreliable. The internal API was clean and stable. The endpoint returned 21 restaurants total (TotalCount appears to be the full ranked list for this dish). 'rank' was assigned by API order (which sorts by AggregatedScore desc). The displayed TasteAtlas 1-5 user score is NOT in this endpoint - only the internal weighted 'AggregatedScore' (raw integer, max observed 37200 for the top entry). 'score_1to5' is a rough proxy (raw/10000) and should not be confused with the public TasteAtlas star rating. Latitude/Longitude were null in this endpoint. Restaurants 19-21 (Sal & Carmine, L'Industrie, The Big Slice) have AggregatedScore <100 because they're user-submitted entries with no critic recommendations yet. No anti-bot block was hit on the API; Cloudflare Turnstile is loaded on page chrome but did not gate the JSON endpoint.

places.name: Totonno's Pizzeria Napolitana
places.address: 1524 Neptune Ave, Brooklyn, NY 11224, USA
places.top_critic_quote: Coal-fired blisters, spotted pools of house-made mozzarella and just the right amount of sauce (read: not much at all) keep us coming back to this 91-year-old Coney Island joint.

places.name: Patsy's Pizzeria
places.address: 2287 1st Avenue, New York, NY 10035, USA
places.top_critic_quote: Sold by the slice at a stand-up storefront or by the whole pie in a sit-down restaurant next door, Patsy's pizza is a New York classic. It is the simplest pie imaginable, easy to hoist slice by slice, built on a marvelous thin crust with charred spots all along the edge that have the smoky flavor that only a coal oven delivers.

places.name: Rubirosa Pizza
places.address: 235 Mulberry St, New York, NY 10012, USA
places.top_critic_quote: The classic pie balances a crispy, cracker-thin crust with tart tomato sauce and oven-browned spots of salty, melting mozzarella.

places.name: Joe's Pizza
places.address: 7 Carmine Street, New York, NY 10014, USA
places.top_critic_quote: This pocket-sized pizzeria excels in the quintessential New York slice, with its pliant yet cracker-thin undercarriage, slightly sweet sauce and a blanket of drippy, fresh mozzarella—big rounds of pepperoni are the metaphorical cherry on top.

places.name: Di Fara Pizza
places.address: 1424 Avenue J, Brooklyn, NY 11230, USA
places.top_critic_quote: The best of the best.

places.name: Juliana's
places.address: 19 Old Fulton St, Brooklyn, NY 11201, USA
places.top_critic_quote: Those looking for the authentic Grimaldi's experience really should be hitting up Juliana's where the crust has gained a reputation among some for being more crisp and airy with more complex flavor.

places.name: John's of Bleecker Street
places.address: 278 Bleecker St, New York, NY 10014, USA
places.top_critic_quote: It is a source of true New York-style pizza in a setting that could be nowhere else.

places.name: Joe & Pat's Pizzeria
places.address: 1758 Victory Blvd, Staten Island, NY 10314, USA
places.top_critic_quote: It was founded in 1960 by Joe and Pat Pappalardo, and the most popular pizza has an imperially thin crust, crisper than regular Neapolitan, with a sprightly tomato sauce and memorably good mozzarella.

places.name: Ernesto's Pizza
places.address: 69 Salem St, Boston, MA 02113, USA
places.top_critic_quote: These Are 16 of Boston's Most Iconic Pizzerias: Ernesto's Pizza - When ordering, be aware that "one slice" is actually a quarter of a pizza.

places.name: Louie & Ernie's Pizza
places.address: 1300 Crosby Ave, Bronx, NY 10461, USA
places.top_critic_quote: It's a hot, creamy mess of pizza-and-pork deliciousness.

places.name: Lombardi's Pizza
places.address: 32 Spring St, New York, NY 10012, USA
places.top_critic_quote: Since 1897, Lombardi's has been winning over New Yorkers like Rachael Ray with their perfect balance of marinara and mozzarella, all atop a thin crust baked in a coal-fired oven.

places.name: Home Slice Pizza
places.address: 1415 S Congress Ave, Austin, TX 78704, USA
places.top_critic_quote: Home Slice draws the Austin crowds with its New York-style pizzas, which have proven to be so popular that the joint sells 1,000 pies daily.

places.name: Za Pizza
places.address: 1919 Hyde St, San Francisco, CA 94109, USA
places.top_critic_quote: It's thin crust, but it doesn't skimp on the toppings. Flavor where flavor is due, this spot piles them on unapologetically. The walls are covered in fan art, which should be taken as a rightful indication of how beloved their pies are.

places.name: Grimaldi's
places.address: 1 Front St, Brooklyn, NY 11201, USA
places.top_critic_quote: Not only will you get a memorable pie, you'll also get a memorable view of Manhattan from one of the oldest — and most picturesque — parts of Brooklyn.

places.name: Vezzo Thin Crust Pizza
places.address: 178 Lexington Ave, New York, NY 10016, USA
places.top_critic_quote: I walk past every day and decided to try, and it did not disappoint! Super thin crunchy crust! Really worth it!

places.name: Lucali
places.address: 575 Henry St, Brooklyn, NY 11231, USA
places.top_critic_quote: The pies, topped with basil, garlic, pepperoni, and vegetables like grilled artichokes, are often ranked as some of the best in New York City.

places.name: Pizza Delicious
places.address: 617 Piety St, New Orleans, LA 70117, USA
places.top_critic_quote: The thin-crust pies here are done New York-style and taste great. The preparation is simple, but the ingredients are fresh and consistently top-notch.

places.name: Giampietro Pasta & Pizzeria
places.address: 100 N Main St #212, Breckenridge, CO 80424, USA
places.top_critic_quote: Dig into some consistently good, honest and soulful New York-style pizza.

places.name: Sal & Carmine Pizza
places.address: 2671 Broadway, New York, NY 10025, USA
places.top_critic_quote: Sal & Carmine sets the standard for a classic New York-style slice and has been a mainstay of the Upper West Side since 1959 — for good reason.

places.name: L'Industrie Pizzeria
places.address: 254 S 2nd St, Brooklyn, NY 11211, USA
places.top_critic_quote: Had their house special and burrata pizza. A good spot hitter and has a unique blend of ny style with their slightly sharp cheese and italiana with the airy crust.

places.name: The Big Slice
places.address: 1454 E Sunshine St, Springfield, MO 65804, USA
places.top_critic_quote: Slices the size of paper plates for around $4. Very tasty and lots of choices.
